actor_relation_claims.role_detail: indicated interest in participating in potential acquisition

quote_texts: Sponsor E had entered into discussions with Sponsor G, a private equity firm, regarding a potential joint acquisition of Saks
Sponsor E would again be joined in its proposal by Sponsor A as a primary participant
Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement
Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.
Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.

Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

Hudson’s Bay’s proposal included a price of $15.25 per share of common stock, a revised draft merger agreement and information and documentation relating to Hudson’s Bay’s committed debt and equity financing for the potential transaction.
The joint proposal from Sponsor E and Sponsor G included an indicative price range of $14.50–$15.50 per share of common stock

Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.
representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock
the $16 per share of common stock in cash to be paid to Saks’ shareholders in the proposed merger

event_claims.description: Sponsor A made an unsolicited contact expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.
quote_texts: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Richard Baker requested a meeting with Mr. Sadove and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.
quote_texts: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: The board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with a potential acquiror that had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached.
quote_texts: authorized (1) the implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors which had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached on terms that would offer attractive value to Saks shareholders

event_claims.description: At Saks’ direction, Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E requesting all-cash acquisition offers by July 11, 2013.
quote_texts: On July 2, 2013, at the direction of Saks, Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E, with a request for submission of offers for an all-cash acquisition of Saks, along with comments on the draft merger agreement, no later than July 11, 2013.

event_claims.description: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.
quote_texts: On July 8, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
quote_texts: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Sponsor E together with Sponsor G submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
quote_texts: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Saks was informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process.
quote_texts: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process

event_claims.description: The board authorized Goldman Sachs to tell Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor E and Sponsor A that their initial price proposals were insufficient.
quote_texts: The board authorized Goldman Sachs to inform each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E and Sponsor A, on the other hand, that their initial price proposals were insufficient

event_claims.description: Saks received a letter from Company H purporting to propose to acquire Saks for $2.6 billion in cash.
quote_texts: On July 21, 2013, Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.

event_claims.description: Goldman Sachs was unsuccessful in contacting Company H, and neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.
quote_texts: Goldman Sachs subsequently attempted on more than one occasion to contact the appropriate person at Company H both by telephone and by e-mail to discuss the purported offer further but was unsuccessful in making contact with such person. Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that it was prepared to offer $16 per share, subject to negotiating a definitive binding agreement.
quote_texts: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.
quote_texts: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.

event_claims.actor_label: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub
event_claims.description: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents.
quote_texts: Following the board’s approval of the merger and the merger agreement, Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.

Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).
On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
